adapter_contract.notes: Full beginner grammar lesson 01 preview aligned to modular source content.

title_i18n.zh_tw: Beginner Grammar Lesson 01 · 第一次見面先把語氣說對
title_i18n.en: Beginner Grammar Lesson 01 · Choose the right speech level in a first meeting

theme_i18n.zh_tw: 正式 / 日常 / 朋友語氣

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 能先分出正式、日常、朋友三種說法
can_do_i18n.en: Tell formal, everyday, and casual speech apart.

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 能把同一句切成不同語氣版本
can_do_i18n.en: Switch the same sentence into different speech levels.

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 能在第一次見面先穩定用 해요체 開口
can_do_i18n.en: Start a first meeting safely with 해요체.

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 能避開 저/나、예요/이에요 的初級常錯
can_do_i18n.en: Avoid beginner mistakes with 저/나 and 예요/이에요.

what_to_notice_i18n.zh_tw: 先不要急著背整句，先盯住句尾：`입니다 / 예요 / 야`。
what_to_notice_i18n.en: Do not try to memorize the full sentence first. Focus on the ending: `입니다 / 예요 / 야`.

what_to_notice_i18n.zh_tw: 這一節要先聽出：同樣是「我是 Yuna」，語尾一換，禮貌度就跟著換。
what_to_notice_i18n.en: In this section, hear how the same idea changes in politeness when the ending changes.

what_to_notice_i18n.zh_tw: 先把語尾禮貌度聽開，比一開始硬背規則更重要。
what_to_notice_i18n.en: At this stage, hearing the politeness level in the ending matters more than memorizing rules.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽比較正式的說法
title_i18n.en: Hear `입니다` first in a first-meeting setting

note_i18n.zh_tw: 這種句尾比較正式，先認得出來就好。
note_i18n.en: You do not need the most ceremonial greeting first. Just hear how `입니다` sounds.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽日常最常用的說法
title_i18n.en: Use `요` in everyday conversation

note_i18n.zh_tw: 平常最常聽到的是這種，第一次開口先用這個最安全。
note_i18n.en: This is the most common one, so get used to how it sounds.

translations_i18n.en: Oh, really? Nice to meet you.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 再聽朋友之間怎麼說
title_i18n.en: Hear `야` between close friends

note_i18n.zh_tw: 這種只在熟人之間用，先會分辨就好。
note_i18n.en: For now, use this to recognize the sound. Do not start with it when talking to strangers.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 先記這三個就夠
title_i18n.en: These three are enough for now

why_here_i18n.zh_tw: 這一課先不要想太複雜。先記：正式場合用 `입니다`，日常對話用 `요`，朋友互動用 `야`。
why_here_i18n.en: Do not overcomplicate this lesson. Start with this: use `입니다` in formal situations, `요` in everyday conversation, and `야` with close friends.

explain_i18n.en: Use this first in formal situations.
This is the most common and safest everyday choice.
This is common between close friends.

dialogue_scene_contract.purpose: L1 remains fixed input for learners, but each line must stay reusable for G1, practice, and review generation.

title_i18n.zh_tw: L1 先聽語尾：三種禮貌程度差在哪
title_i18n.en: L1 Listen first: how the three speech styles feel different

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽三小段就好。這一節的重點不是背句子，而是先聽出 `입니다 / 예요 / 야` 這些語尾怎麼把禮貌程度拉開。
summary_i18n.en: Start with three very short dialogues. Do not memorize them yet. Just hear how formal, everyday, and close-friend speech feel different.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能先聽出同一句在正式、日常、朋友三種語尾下的禮貌程度差別。
expected_output_i18n.en: Start hearing formal, everyday, and close-friend speech as different modes.

intro_i18n.zh_tw: 先看熟這三種句子就好。生活裡真的很常用。
intro_i18n.en: Just get familiar with these three sentence types first. They show up a lot in real life.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句子 1：這是什麼
title_i18n.en: Sentence 1: What is this?

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 先看「這 / 那 / 那邊」怎麼一起變。
inline_hint_i18n.en: Watch how “this / that / that over there” shift together.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句子 2：現在幾點
title_i18n.en: Sentence 2: What time is it now?

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 這句很常用，而且正式、日常、朋友三種說法都很清楚。
inline_hint_i18n.en: This sentence is very common, and the formal, polite, and casual versions are easy to compare.

title_i18n.zh_tw: L2 先看看：最常碰到的兩種句子
title_i18n.en: L2 Take a look: two sentence types you meet all the time

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 這裡先看兩種最常碰到的句子：指東西、問時間。
summary_i18n.en: Start with two very common sentence types: pointing at things and asking the time.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能先看懂「這是什麼」和「現在幾點」怎麼換說法。
expected_output_i18n.en: Recognize how “what is this” and “what time is it now” change across speech styles.

intro_i18n.zh_tw: 動詞也會跟著換語氣。先選動詞，再換它常接的地點、交通、吃喝和活動。
intro_i18n.en: Verb sentences change too. Pick a verb first, then swap the place, transport, food, drink, or activity that goes with it.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句子 1：動詞也會跟著換，後面的名詞也能換
title_i18n.en: Sentence 1: Verb endings change too, and the phrase after them can change as well

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 先選動詞，再換它平常會接的地方、交通、吃喝和活動。
inline_hint_i18n.en: Pick a verb first, then switch among the place, transport, food, drink, or activity that normally goes with it.

title_i18n.zh_tw: L3 再看一組：基本動詞也會跟著換
title_i18n.en: L3 One more set: core verbs change too

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 這裡把基本動詞拉出來看。去哪裡、搭什麼、吃喝什麼，都能跟著語氣一起換。
summary_i18n.en: Now look at core verbs on their own. Going somewhere, taking transport, eating, and drinking all change with the speech level.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能切出幾個最常用的動詞句，並換不同的地點、交通和食物飲料。
expected_output_i18n.en: Build a few very common verb sentences and swap the place, transport, food, or drink that goes with them.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句型 1：同一句先切三種語氣
title_i18n.en: Pattern 1: Switch one sentence across three speech levels

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 先從這一句開始，最容易抓到三種說法的差別。
inline_hint_i18n.en: Start with this one. It is the easiest way to feel the difference across the three styles.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句型 2：問時間也一起換語氣
title_i18n.en: Pattern 2: Change the speech level when asking the time

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 這句很常用，最適合拿來練正式、日常、朋友三種差別。
inline_hint_i18n.en: This is a very common sentence, so it is a good one for comparing the three speech levels.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句型 3：同樣的判斷句，也能跟著換語氣
title_i18n.en: Pattern 3: The same statement can also switch speech level

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 說地方時也會一起換，不只自我介紹才會變。
inline_hint_i18n.en: Place sentences change too, not only self-introductions.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 句型 4：動詞句尾也會一起換
title_i18n.en: Pattern 4: Verb endings change too

inline_hint_i18n.zh_tw: 不要只看名詞句，動詞也要一起看正式、日常、朋友三種變化。
inline_hint_i18n.en: Do not stop at noun sentences. Verb endings also change across the three speech levels.

title_i18n.zh_tw: G1 自己切一次：同一句怎麼換說法
title_i18n.en: G1 Try it yourself: how the same sentence changes

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 這裡開始自己動手。先換幾個最常用的詞，再看名詞句和動詞句怎麼切成正式、日常、朋友三種說法。
summary_i18n.en: Now you start doing it yourself. Swap a few high-frequency words and see how the same sentence changes into formal, everyday, and casual speech.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能用同一組材料，切出正式、日常、朋友三種版本。
expected_output_i18n.en: Use the same set of materials to produce formal, polite, and casual versions.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 1. 先抓這一個重點
title_i18n.en: 1. What to learn first in this lesson

explanation_md_i18n.zh_tw:
先別把這課想成什麼「韓語敬語大全」。那樣一開始壓力太大。

你現在先抓住一件事就夠了：**同一句話，會因為你在跟誰說話，句尾跟著換。**

這課先只管三種最常先碰到的說法：

- `~습니다 / ~ㅂ니다`：比較正式
- `~아요 / ~어요 / ~해요`：平常最常用，也最安全
- `~야 / ~이야`：熟了之後才比較自然的朋友說法

所以你現在真的不用急著背一堆術語。先養成一個反應就好：**我現在是在跟誰講話？**
explanation_md_i18n.en:
This lesson is **not** trying to teach the whole Korean honorific system at once.

Start with one practical idea: **the ending changes depending on who you are speaking to**.

Lesson 01 only covers the three speech endings you will meet first: formal `~습니다 / ~ㅂ니다`, everyday polite `~아요 / ~어요 / ~해요`, and casual `~야 / ~이야`.

The core goal is not memorizing terminology. It is learning to ask yourself: **Who am I speaking to right now?**

title_i18n.zh_tw: 2. 三種說法先怎麼認
title_i18n.en: 2. How the three styles differ

points_i18n.zh_tw: `~습니다 / ~ㅂ니다`：你可以先把它聽成「比較正式的版本」。櫃台、公告、面試、比較正式的自我介紹，很常聽到這種感覺。
points_i18n.en: `~습니다 / ~ㅂ니다`: the most formal. Common at counters, in notices, interviews, formal self-introductions, and broadcast-style speech.

points_i18n.zh_tw: `~아요 / ~어요 / ~해요`：這個你先當主力就好。第一次見面、旅行、問路、買東西、跟不熟的人講話，大多先用這個最穩。
points_i18n.en: `~아요 / ~어요 / ~해요`: the most common everyday choice. Use this first in travel, first meetings, asking for directions, shopping, and conversations with people you do not know well.

points_i18n.zh_tw: `~야 / ~이야`：這個不是不能學，是先拿來認得出來。真的對熟朋友、家人、晚輩再用比較自然；一開始就對剛認識的人用，通常會太快。
points_i18n.en: `~야 / ~이야`: casual friend speech. Usually for close friends, family, or younger people. Using this right away with a newly met adult is usually too fast.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 3. 先練哪一個最划算
title_i18n.en: 3. Which one to remember first

explanation_md_i18n.zh_tw:
如果你現在才剛開始，真的不用三種平均用力。

比較划算的順序是：

1. **先把 `~아요/어요/해요` 練穩**
2. 再慢慢聽得出 `~습니다/ㅂ니다` 比較正式
3. 最後先做到「看到 `~야/이야` 不會慌」就夠了

為什麼這樣排？因為 `~요` 體就是你最早真的會拿去生活裡用的版本。你在 L1 聽到中間那段最自然，原因就在這裡。
explanation_md_i18n.en:
If you are a beginner, do not train all three evenly first.

A more useful order is:

1. **Stabilize `~아요/어요/해요` first**
2. Learn to recognize that `~습니다/ㅂ니다` sounds more formal
3. Only then get used to seeing `~야/이야`; you do not need to use it on strangers right away

Why? Because the `~요` style is the version you will actually use first in real life.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 4. 這幾個地方最容易卡住
title_i18n.en: 4. The parts people mix up most easily

points_i18n.zh_tw: `저` vs `나`：先簡單記就好。`저` 比較客氣，第一次見面先拿這個；`나` 比較近，多半跟朋友語氣一起出現。
points_i18n.en: `저` vs `나`: `저` is more polite and is the safer first choice in new meetings; `나` is closer and usually appears in casual speech.

points_i18n.zh_tw: `예요` vs `이에요`：這個超常卡。沒有收音大多用 `예요`，有收音用 `이에요`。先記這條就很夠用了。
points_i18n.en: `예요` vs `이에요`: this is one of the most common beginner mistakes in noun sentences. Use `예요` after vowel-ending nouns and `이에요` after batchim nouns.

points_i18n.zh_tw: `가요 / 가`、`읽어요 / 읽어`：不要以為只有名詞句會變。動詞句尾也會一起換，這就是我們後面為什麼又拉一組動詞出來看。
points_i18n.en: `가요 / 가`, `읽어요 / 읽어`: verb endings change too, not only noun sentences.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 5. 你就把它當同一句的三個版本
title_i18n.en: 5. Contrast set: the same sentence, only the speech level changes

explanation_md_i18n.zh_tw:
| 場景感 | 韓文 | 中文 |
|---|---|---|
| 比較正式 | `저는 학생입니다.` | 我是學生。 |
| 平常最好用 | `저는 학생이에요.` | 我是學生。 |
| 熟朋友 | `나는 학생이야.` | 我是學生。 |

你現在先不要把它想成三條分開背的文法。**就把它當同一句在不同距離下換三個版本**，比較好記，也比較像真的在講話。
explanation_md_i18n.en:
| Social distance | Korean | English |
|---|---|---|
| Very formal | `저는 학생입니다.` | I am a student. |
| Everyday safe choice | `저는 학생이에요.` | I am a student. |
| Close-friend style | `나는 학생이야.` | I am a student. |

Do not memorize these as three unrelated grammar items. Think of them as **three versions of the same sentence at different social distances**.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 6. 先不要往太深的地方鑽
title_i18n.en: 6. What you do not need to dig into yet

points_i18n.zh_tw: `-세요 / -으세요` 這種更細的敬語命令 / 請求形式，現在先不要管。
points_i18n.en: More specific polite imperative/request forms like `-세요 / -으세요` are not part of Lesson 01 yet.

points_i18n.zh_tw: 主體敬語 `-시-/-으시-`、客體敬語、尊稱詞彙，後面再慢慢進就好。
points_i18n.en: Subject honorific `-시-/-으시-`, object honorifics, and honorific vocabulary come later.

points_i18n.zh_tw: 這課先專心練「我現在該用哪種語氣」。先把這個練穩，負擔會小很多。
points_i18n.en: For now, stay focused on “which speech level do I use with this listener?” This keeps the load manageable.

title_i18n.zh_tw: 7. 這課先把這三句記在腦子裡
title_i18n.en: 7. Take these three lines with you first

points_i18n.zh_tw: 第一次見面拿不準時，先用 `~아요/어요/해요`，通常不會錯。
points_i18n.en: When you are unsure in a first meeting, start with `~아요/어요/해요`.

points_i18n.zh_tw: 朋友說法不是不能學，是不要太早拿去對陌生人用。
points_i18n.en: Casual friend speech is not wrong; it is just too early for strangers.

points_i18n.zh_tw: 這課不是叫你背術語，是先養成「看對象選句尾」的習慣。
points_i18n.en: This lesson is not about memorizing terminology; it is about building the habit of choosing endings based on the person in front of you.

title_i18n.zh_tw: G2 先把這三種說法放對地方
title_i18n.en: G2 Clear explanation: when to use which style

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 這裡不講太多術語，先把 `입니다`、`요`、`야` 大概各用在哪裡放對。
summary_i18n.en: This section explains the key decisions in this lesson: when to use `입니다`, when to use `요`, and when to switch to `야`.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能自己說出：第一次見面為什麼先用 `요`，還有 `의사예요` 為什麼不是 `의사이에요`。
expected_output_i18n.en: Say why `요` is the safe first-meeting choice, and why `의사예요` is not `의사이에요`.

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：上台自我介紹，比較正式
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：我是 Yuna。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟新認識的人聊天，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：我是敏秀。
請組出最自然的一句：我是敏秀。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：第一次見面，日常最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：很高興認識你。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟老師或店員說話，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：這是蘋果。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：櫃台或廣播，比較正式
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：現在幾點？

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟新認識的人聊天，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：我是學生。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟新認識的人聊天，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：這裡是咖啡廳。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟同學或同事聊天，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：去咖啡廳。
請組出最自然的一句：去咖啡廳。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：跟同學或同事聊天，最常用
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：喝咖啡。

tasks.context_zh_tw: 場合：上課報告或播報，比較正式
tasks.prompt_zh_tw: 請組出最自然的一句：讀書。
請組出最自然的一句：運動。

title_i18n.zh_tw: P1 組句練習：看場合，選對語尾再組句
title_i18n.en: P1 Sentence Builder: use the situation to choose the right ending

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 每題都會先告訴你場合。組句時不只要拼對內容，還要從多個語尾裡選出最合適的那一個。
summary_i18n.en: Each task gives a situation. Build the sentence and choose the ending that fits the situation.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能一邊組句，一邊把 `입니다 / 요 / 야` 放回對的場合。
expected_output_i18n.en: Assemble sentences while placing `입니다 / 요 / 야` into the right situation.

payload.hint_policy: 先自己想，卡住再看提示。
reference_answers_ko: 저는 회사원입니다. / 저는 회사원예요. / 나는 회사원이야.
동사도 같이 바뀌어요. 예를 들면 가요 / 가, 읽어요 / 읽어.

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 第一次見面時，你最先該用哪一種句尾？為什麼？
prompts_i18n.en: Which ending should you use first in a first meeting, and why?

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 把「我是上班族」切成正式、日常、朋友三種說法。
prompts_i18n.en: Turn “I am an office worker” into formal, everyday, and casual versions.

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 動詞句也會跟著變。請舉一組例子。
prompts_i18n.en: Verb sentences change too. Give one example.

title_i18n.zh_tw: R1 課程總結：先自己回想這一課
title_i18n.en: R1 Lesson Wrap-Up: recall this lesson on your own first

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 先不要急著看提示，先自己把這一課最重要的內容想一遍。
summary_i18n.en: Before checking hints, recall the most important parts of this lesson on your own.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能先不看提示，自己說出這一課的重點、語尾差別和一組動詞例句。
expected_output_i18n.en: Recall the lesson focus, ending differences, and one verb example before checking hints.

payload.hint_policy: 先看正面，先自己想，再翻頁看答案。

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽這句，想想它是什麼意思。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen to this sentence first and think about what it means.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 第一次見面先用 해요체 最安全。
note_i18n.en: In a first meeting, 해요체 is the safest starting point.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽這句，想想它在問什麼。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first and think about what this question is asking.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 這句很適合練語氣切換：입니까 / 예요 / 야。
note_i18n.en: This is a good sentence for practicing 입니까 / 예요 / 야.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽句子，再想完整意思。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen to the sentence first, then think of the full meaning.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 名詞在選項裡先看本體，句子裡再補助詞。
note_i18n.en: Show the noun stem in the selector first, then add the particle in the sentence.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽句子，再想它在說什麼。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first, then think about what the sentence says.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽這句，再想想它比較像什麼場合。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first and think about what situation this sounds like.

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 我是學生。這句比較正式。
answer_i18n.en: I am a student. This sounds more formal.

note_i18n.zh_tw: `입니다` 文法沒問題，但日常第一次見面更常先聽到 `학생이에요`。
note_i18n.en: `입니다` is correct, but in everyday first meetings `학생이에요` is more common.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽這句，再想想它像在跟誰說話。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first and think about who this sounds like it is for.

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 我是敏秀。這句是朋友語氣。
answer_i18n.en: I am Minsu. This is a casual friend-style sentence.

note_i18n.zh_tw: `야` 放在熟朋友之間最自然，不要太早拿去對陌生人用。
note_i18n.en: `야` is most natural with close friends, not with strangers.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽句子，再判斷它比較正式還是日常。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first, then decide whether this sounds formal or everyday.

answer_i18n.en: Read a book. This sounds more formal.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 同一句如果換成日常最常用，會是 `책을 읽어요`。
note_i18n.en: The everyday version of the same sentence is `책을 읽어요`.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽句子，再想想它像是哪種距離感。
prompt_i18n.en: Listen first, then think about what level of closeness this suggests.

answer_i18n.en: Exercise. This is a casual friend-style sentence.

note_i18n.en: In casual speech, `하다` often changes directly to `해`.

title_i18n.zh_tw: P2 卡片練習：先想，再翻頁看答案
title_i18n.en: P2 Flashcard Practice: recall first, then flip for the answer

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 先看句子，先自己想意思或場合，再翻頁檢查，最後選熟練度。
summary_i18n.en: See the sentence, recall the meaning or situation first, then flip and rate your recall.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能用卡片快速複習這課的核心句型與語尾差別。
expected_output_i18n.en: Use flashcards to review the core sentences and ending differences from this lesson.
